zh.lifeSummary: 當John Thomas de Ashton於1403年出生在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林時，他的父親John de Ashton61歲，母親Jane Savile31歲。他於1417年與Elizabeth de Byron在英國英格蘭藍卡爾艾希頓 武安德 林結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1446年被列為english alchemist。他去世於1468年9月3日在他的家鄉享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Thomas de Ashton于1403年出生在英国藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林时，他的父亲John de Ashton61岁，母亲Jane Savile31岁。他于1417年与Elizabeth de Byron在英国英格兰藍卡爾艾希頓 武安德 林结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1446年被列为english alchemist。他去世于1468年9月3日在他的家乡享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ashton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called, especially Ashton-under-Lyne near Manchester. Most are named from Old English æsc ‘ash tree’ + tūn ‘settlement’; the one in Northamptonshire is (æt thǣm) æscum ‘(at the) ash trees’. Others have been assimilated to this from different sources. The one in Devon is ‘the settlement (tūn) of Æschere’, while the one in Hertfordshire is ‘the settlement of Ælli’.

variants.name: Aspden
meanings: English: habitational name from Aspden (alias Aspen) in Oswaldtwistle, Lancashire. This surname has been confused with   Ashton   and   Austin  , and probably also with   Aston   and   Astin  , all of which are common surnames in Lancashire.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Thomas de Ashton was born in 1403, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, his father, John de Ashton, was 61 and his mother, Jane Savile, was 31. He married Elizabeth de Byron in 1417, in Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. In 1446, at the age of 43, his occupation is listed as english alchemist. He died on 3 September 1468, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

Il a épousé Elizabeth de Byron en 1417, à Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. En 1446, à l’âge de 43 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est english alchemist. Il est décédé le 3 septembre 1468, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: John Thomas de Ashtonが1403年にイギリスランカシュアイアアシュトン-ウンデル-リンで生まれた時，父John de Ashtonは61歳，母Jane Savileは31歳であった。1417年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアアシュトン ウンダー リンでElizabeth de Byronと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1446年，43歳の時，職業はenglish alchemistと記録されている。彼は1468年9月3日に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Thomas de Ashton(이)가 1403년 영국 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Ashton(은)는 61세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Savile(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1417년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 애슈턴 운더 리니에서 Elizabeth de Byron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1446년, 43세의 나이로 그의 직업은 english alchemist이었다. 그는 1468년 9월 3일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Byron в 1417 г. в местности аштон ундир лин, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. В 1446 году, в возрасте 43 лет,  он работал по профессии english alchemist. Он умер 3 сентября 1468 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.
